test_cases.input: Documentary about a community of deaf surfers in a coastal town who compete nationally. The film follows three surfers over one competitive season. Observational style. Festival submission and broadcaster pitch needed.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 5 loglines including: 'Three deaf surfers. One competitive season. A film about what the ocean sounds like when you can't hear it.' (poetic/festival), 'An observational film following three deaf competitive surfers through one national season — and the question of whether the sport was built for them or despite them.' (broadcaster). All options had distinct emphases. Social short-form included. Annotations accurate.

test_cases.input: Documentary about the history of a specific architectural style (brutalism) and its contested preservation. Multi-subject, no single protagonist. Public broadcaster context. Polemical tone.
test_cases.output_summary: Correctly flagged that without a protagonist, the dramatic spine is the debate itself. Generated loglines structured around the central conflict ('Who decides which buildings survive?') rather than a character arc. Thematic form worked best. One option slightly too academic for the broadcaster context — annotated as such. Social bio line effective.

test_cases.input: Documentary following a small family-owned publishing house in its final year before closing. Three employees, the owner, and the last books they will publish. Intimate, observational. International film festival submission.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 5 loglines. Best: 'A small publishing house is closing. This is its last year, its last books, and the people who stayed until the end.' Clean, specific, earned. Festival long-form included context on the industry angle. Social bio line under 20 words. All options distinguished by emphasis. No option was generic.
